domain: Brazilian capoeira ginga rhythmic baseline (continuous A-B-C sway is the foundation; every attack launches from a ginga phase and returns to ginga; berimbau-led music dictates tempo; capoeirista is never static — always in ginga)
specific_mechanism: Ginga = continuous swaying footwork pattern that is the baseline state. From any ginga step the practitioner can launch an attack (martelo, meia-lua) or defense (cocorinha, esquiva) and return to ginga. The ginga is the launch-platform AND the idle state. Berimbau rhythm tempo dictates ginga speed; capoeirista's ginga synchronises to the music.
llm_application: Ginga-baseline background idle reasoning loop: when LLM is at low load between user requests, instead of being silent, run a continuous background loop that auto-completes 'what background fact about user/context might be relevant later?' over a rolling buffer at very low temperature, storing outputs in a background-reasoning queue. When a user request arrives, response generation LAUNCHES from current background-reasoning state (not from scratch). Properties: (1) warm-up cost amortised across idle time; (2) ambient context-anticipation; (3) tempo-aware: background ginga rate scales to user idle duration; (4) responsive: background state is always launch-ready.
novelty_claim: LLMs-Have-Rhythm (2502.20589) is fingerprinting via timing, not idle baseline. RHYTHM (2509.23115) is hierarchical temporal tokenization for time-series, not idle reasoning. Speculative decoding works on next-token prediction during active generation, not background loops between requests. The ginga discipline (continuous background reasoning during idle + launch from background buffer on request) is not in surveyed prior art.
